symbols: armed warriors — the mighty men of David's army
description: A medieval panel painting showing two armed men in period armor, likely representing the biblical warriors Sabobai and Benaiah from David's mighty men. They stand as half-length portraits in rich clothing with weapons.
theological_description: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was one of David's mighty men, renowned for his exploits: 'He struck down two heroes of Moab. He also went down and struck down a lion in a pit on a day when snow had fallen' (2SA.23.20). These warriors represent the human courage empowered by God that characterized David's kingdom.

scripture_refs.reason: Benaiah's heroic deeds as one of David's mighty men
